denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/17/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102565756          Group #: GRP-1324
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-148409
Claim #: CLM-2026-205173
Date of Service (requested): 03/28/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/30/2026 for MRI lumbar spine without contrast related to Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
Diagnosis (as submitted): Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM104 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This request has been processed under a benefit category that does not provide coverage for the requested service (benefit category classification).
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM104 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. UHC medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before MRI lumbar spine without contrast. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26).

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102565756) and reference UM-2026-148409. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). He has been treated under commercial coverage with MRI lumbar spine without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. Radicular pain 6 weeks; failed PT; straight-leg raise positive. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that step therapy missing bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
